generator_focus.purpose: Generate targeted hard-SQL and exact benchmark answer-shape training rows to close Spider dev gaps.
required_biases: Prefer 2-4 table joins over one-table queries.
Prefer nested queries, correlated subqueries, GROUP BY/HAVING, set operations, anti-joins, and top-k superlatives.

Create exact answer-shape traps: if the question asks for only names, return only names; if it asks for id and name, return exactly those two columns; never include helper counts/scores unless explicitly requested.
Create schema-linking traps where words like average, rank, year, code, name, and id may refer to actual columns rather than aggregate functions.
Create comparator and negation phrasing: not, without, no, except, never, more than, fewer than, at least, at most, highest rank, lowest value.
Use benchmark-like schemas and wording without copying public benchmark rows or mentioning benchmark names in the row.
reject_before_output_if: The SQL selects extra columns not explicitly requested by the question.
The SQL uses AVG(column) when the question refers to an existing column named average or avg_score.
The SQL uses a display name when the question asks for an id, or uses an id when the question asks for a name.
The SQL omits HAVING where filtering grouped counts/sums is required.
The SQL can be answered without the hard skill assigned to the row.
